name: Iron Caldera
description: A civilization that embodies unyielding defiance against the chaos of Kael, The Iron Hide, through meticulous engineering and resilience.

hierarchy.description: The ultimate authority within the Iron Caldera, chosen for their proven wisdom, skill, and endurance.
The primary architects and builders of the Caldera, responsible for the structural integrity and defensive fortification of Forgespire.
The primary engineers and miners, responsible for extracting rare metals and powering the Caldera's industry.

units.name: Shelled Reptiles
units.description: Immense, spiked tortoises that form a literal spear wall, absorbing and repelling frontal assaults.

units.name: Salamander-like Lizards
units.description: Tall, slender lizards that serve as cavalry, vanguards, archers, and gunners, striking with speed and agility.

units.name: Alchemical Warfare Corps
units.description: Positioned behind the main defensive line, providing fire support with alchemical grenades and specialized launchers.

units.name: Hatchery Nurses
units.description: Tenders of the hatcheries, trained in natural poisons, hidden claws and spikes, and unconventional warfare.

units.name: Forge Dancers
units.description: Elite warriors who guard vital locations and are seemingly invulnerable, their combat style a fluid, instantly deadly dance.

rituals.name: Festival of Life (Shadow Week)
rituals.description: A massive celebration of defiance during the global seven-day eclipse, where the Ironborn seal the great gates and consume vast stockpiles of food and resources.

rituals.name: Rite of Passage
rituals.description: A brutal test of endurance and survival, where a group of young Lizardlings must find their way back to the citadel, either all together or not at all.

economy.internal_sustenance: Massive, subterranean farms cultivating specialized fungus, hardy desert plants, and vast quantities of farmed bugs and grubs.
economy.trade: Designated 'Ironless' Outposts, where they trade their superior metalwork and clockwork technology for 'luxury' foods and other goods.
